title: Level 164
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 164: "Structures in the Dark"}} {{Rewrite In Progress|date=June 6, 2026|reason=Most stuff in this page is quite underdeveloped. Some really cool stuff like sidewalks to the voidy sky is cool but could use more information. Lastly, the entities in the level kinda ruin the tension. I think it would be more creepy with no entities. These add almost nothing and the environment itself is much scarier.|author= Arkansas and Terraria|rewriter=Sammy|progress=[[User:Sammoyoyoyo/164RW|✦]]}} {{Background|file=114-1.webp}} {{Image Banner|image=114-2.webp|caption=It's what is in the dark that you're afraid of.}} {{Alt Class|no=164|ext=2|env=2|ent=2}} '''Level 164''' is the 165th level of the Backrooms. == Description == {{Blurimage|caption=A common image of Level 164.|file=114-1.webp|hovertext=Sidewalk ends.}} {{Blurimage|caption=A photo of Level 164's structures.|file=114-2.webp|hovertext=Stretching into the dark.}}'''Level 164''', otherwise known as '''Structures in the Dark''', is a large, mostly flat plane of land, roads, and miscellaneous buildings scattered across its entire surface. The level has been thought to be directly connected to [[The Void]], with few disputing that '''Level 164''' should be a void object instead of a standard level. Notably, the level seems to shift at random intervals, with sections of the level swapping with other sectionsSome people have stated that this swapping is analogous to swapping puzzle pieces. without warning. Despite this, the shifting only occurs when no one is in the immediate area, or is able to witness the shift occur. The structures within '''Level 164''' have little variety, with the most common being housesNotably one story in height, two stories are less common but not hard to find. with wooden exteriors, white-painted walls, and dysfunctional interior lighting. Variations of these features are less common, although may still be encountered. Other structures such as gas stations and small apartment complexes are rare among the majority of houses, with all documented appearing completely identical. The roads of the level are gray, dry, and slightly cracked, seeming to be randomly placed with abrupt dead-ends, useless cul-de-sacs, and random crosswalks. The lighting in '''Level 164''' is purely by streetlights, said lights being around ten feet in height, and with the poles being made out of pure solidified gallium, this gallium is unable to be melted for unknown reasons. The average temperature in the level ranges around 40-50 degrees Fahrenheit, although it has a complete lack of any wind, wanderers have noted a hot breeze washing over them at random times while exploring the level. As mentioned previously, the level is thought to be connected to the Void, this is because it has a complete lack of a skybox, as the sky extends indefinitely. Some people have even claimed that '''Level 164''' is the "floor" of the Void. Other, less important details of the level, are details of '''Level 164''' that bare no importance to any sort of survival in the level. Such details are the following; *The grass in the level is fake, instead made of a plastic-like material. * Street signs are found across the level, although they are usually random, such as one in the middle of a road. *Some patches of the level have piles of mulch on the ground. *Sidewalks can be seen going straight up into the sky. ===The Bridge=== {{Blurimage|caption=A picture of the Bridge.|file=114-5.jpg|hovertext=A constant presence.}} A structure in '''Level 164''', aptly named "The Bridge" is a structure that is partially separated from the rest of the level. The Bridge appears as a two-lane road with columns on the bottom, the Bridge lacks pile footing, as the columns end at around fifty feet from the wingwalls. The Bridge can be seen in most parts of '''Level 164''', as it floats above the level at around 2,500 feet from the surfaceIt is notable to mention that the Bridge has an estimated length of 9,000 miles long.. It has been described to move around the level with a slithering-like motion. On extremely rare occasions, the Bridge can lower down to the level, making the columns touch the surface of '''Level 164'''. A disembodied roaring sound effect can be heard from parts of the Bridge. It is theoretically possible to get to the top of the Bridge, as rare few buildings are tall enough for climbing, but the Bridge has not encountered any of these buildings in any recorded times. ===False Lights=== {{Blurimage|caption=A picture of false lights.|file=114-6.webp|hovertext=They aren't real.}} False lights are a phenomenon exclusive to '''Level 164'''. Not much is known about this phenomenon due to its enigmatic nature. False lights usually appear as windows from houses with the lights turned on. If one were to approach these false lights, they would slowly dissipate into the darkness. It is also notable that these false lights do not produce any light to their surroundings. False lights have been appearing in '''Level 164''' at an increasing rate over the past twenty years, it is unknown what this could mean. ==Entities== Entities are relatively common within '''Level 164''', but not enough to pose any great danger to people inside the level. These entities usually include [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], and [[Entity 6|Dullers]]. A select few reports have stated that [[Entity 24|Refuse]] have also lurked inside the level, but these reports have yet to be confirmed. ==Bases, Communities, and Outposts== Due to an extreme lack of supplies, and any desire to, bases, communities, and outposts are absent in '''Level 164'''. ==Entrances and Exits== It is notable to mention that all entrances and exits to and from '''Level 164''' are strictly within the 100-199 level range. ===Entrances=== * Jumping off the edges in [[Level 105]] may lead one to this level. * Entering a nuclear cooling tower painted entirely in black in [[Level 174]] will lead to this level. ===Exits=== * Extremely rare cases of no-clipping into the grass will lead to [[Level 176]]. * It is thought that going high enough into the sky will potentially lead to most likely, [[The Void]]. * Entering a building with all windows shattered will lead to [[Level 188]]. * Entering a building with a sign reading "Closed Store" will lead to [[Level 197]]. * Noclipping through the piles of mulch sends one to [[Level 186]]. * Scarcely, noclipping through second-floor balconies on certain buildings will lead to [[The Balcony]]. ---- {{Footnotes}} ----
